entities.name: INDIA
entities.description: The country where the study on economic growth and regional disparities was conducted

entities.name: ECONOMIC GROWTH
entities.description: The increase in the capacity of an economy to produce goods and services, compared from one period of time to another

entities.name: CONVERGENCE
entities.description: The process where poorer states catch up to richer states in terms of income levels

entities.name: ECONOMIC LIBERALISATION
entities.description: The reduction of government restrictions on economic activities

entities.name: AGRICULTURAL CONDITIONS
entities.description: The state of agriculture that influences the process of economic convergence

entities.name: INCOME LEVELS
entities.description: The amount of money earned by individuals or states, used to measure divergence or convergence

entities.name: STRUCTURAL CHARACTERISTICS
entities.description: The inherent features of states that, when controlled, reveal a tendency toward convergence

entities.name: PHYSICAL INFRASTRUCTURE
entities.description: Basic physical and organizational structures needed for the operation of a society, found to enhance economic growth

entities.name: HUMAN CAPITAL
entities.description: The skills, knowledge, and experience possessed by an individual or population, found to enhance economic growth

entities.name: EMPLOYMENT PRESSURE ON AGRICULTURE
entities.description: The stress placed on the agricultural sector due to high labor demand

entities.name: LABOUR MARKET LINKAGES
entities.description: The connections between agricultural and non-agricultural labor markets

entities.name: LABOUR-INTENSIVE AGRICULTURAL TECHNOLOGIES
entities.description: Agricultural technologies that require a significant amount of labor to promote growth

relationships.description: controlling for these reveals a tendency toward
